search_queries_used: DHA telehealth cough clinical guideline PDF child
DHA telehealth common cold children guideline PDF
population_applicability: Partial: the common-cold source includes children, while the cough source does not state an age limit; neither supports every bronchiolitis or croup item.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact source is limited to DHA-licensed telehealth services.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai and DHA-licensed telehealth only; broader UAE applicability is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Version/Issue 2 copy opened on 2026-07-11; document states revision date 2029-02-21.
superseded_check: DHA official telehealth portal checked on 2026-07-11; no newer official issue was identified there.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Defines the broad Dubai telehealth cough scope and setting limitations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, Dubai purpose, licensed-provider applicability, and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped breathing, cyanosis, systemic, feeding, and worsening concern prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped cough duration, character, timing, risk, and associated-symptom history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports common-cold symptom context and pediatric severity caveats.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies severity-risk contexts and distinguishes uncomplicated common cold from other respiratory presentations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Defines the Dubai common-cold telehealth scope and emergency exclusions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth scope and emergency/controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped pediatric respiratory and swallowing concern prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists respiratory, swallowing, neurologic, rash, and pediatric breathing concerns that require assessment or escalation.

unresolved_source_gaps: The URTI/bronchiolitis diagnosis label and bronchiolitis or croup aliases are not treated as supported by the broad cough and common-cold sections.
Examination, chest imaging, medication, saline, hydration, and fixed follow-up content remains unsupported.
The workflow is broader than DHA telehealth setting.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
